title: brassica oleracea var. villosa
intro: Brassica oleracea var. villosa, commonly known as wild cabbage or sea kale, is a perennial plant that belongs to the Brassicaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The plant is rich in vitamins A, C, and K, as well as minerals such as calcium and iron, making it a valuable source of nutrients with several health benefits, including reducing the risk of heart disease and supporting digestive health. Wild cabbage can be consumed as a leafy green vegetable, and its herbal preparations include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to promote overall well-being and support the immune system.
